headline: Broadway's 'Book of Mormon' Halts Performances After Mysterious Theater Fire
lede: The Tony Award-winning musical comedy grinds to an unexpected halt following significant damage at the Eugene O'Neill Theatre, leaving fans and producers scrambling.
body:
Broadway's long-running satirical sensation 'The Book of Mormon' will remain dark through mid-May after a devastating fire temporarily shuttered the Eugene O'Neill Theatre on West 49th Street.

Producers and venue owner ATG Entertainment announced the immediate cancellation of all performances through May 17, citing ongoing investigation and restoration efforts. The May 4 blaze, whose precise origin remains under investigation, has created significant logistical challenges for the award-winning musical.

The unexpected interruption represents a serious financial and artistic disruption for the musical, which has been a consistent box office powerhouse since its 2011 debut. While structural damage assessments continue, the production's creative team is working closely with local fire and building authorities to determine a precise timeline for resuming performances.

Ticketholders have been advised to contact the box office directly for refunds or alternative performance dates. The musical, created by 'South Park' architects Trey Parker and Matt Stone, has maintained its popularity despite the unexpected setback.

Venue safety protocols will likely receive heightened scrutiny in the wake of this incident, potentially influencing broader Broadway safety standards moving forward.
key_facts: Fire occurred on May 4 at Eugene O'Neill Theatre
Created by Trey Parker and Matt Stone
Cause of fire remains under investigation
